home.title: Start strong with one clear observation
home.body: Start a new observation when you notice a meaningful learning moment.
home.meaning: Capturing evidence in the moment helps you make reliable judgements and plan purposeful next steps.
home.tip: Use one specific example of what the child did, said, or showed, then follow the guided pathway.
home.reassurance: You do not need perfect wording to begin. You can refine and update the report at any time.

default.title: Helpful Tip
default.body: This tool supports your professional judgement. Focus on what you observed, heard, or noticed in the moment.
default.meaning: The KLPT helps you recognise learning and plan next steps; it is not for scoring or comparing children.
default.reassurance: If more than one option feels close, choose the best fit for now. You can always adjust later.
default.tip: Keep your language descriptive and evidence-based.

steps.title: Choose a focus domain
steps.body: Choose the broad area of learning that best connects to what you observed.
steps.meaning: Domains group related capabilities and help frame your observation.
steps.reassurance: If two domains feel relevant, choose the one that felt most central in this moment.
steps.tip: Start with what stood out first.

steps.title: Narrow the focus
steps.body: Choose the subdomain that best matches what you observed.
steps.meaning: Subdomains refine your focus within the broader area of learning.
steps.reassurance: Not every domain includes subdomains. If this step is skipped, continue with confidence.
steps.tip: Think about the type of skill the child was using.

steps.title: Select key elements
steps.body: Choose one or more elements that describe the observed learning.
steps.meaning: Elements represent observable parts of learning within the selected focus.
steps.reassurance: It is okay to choose multiple elements when the observation spans more than one area.
steps.tip: Select based on direct evidence, not expectations.

steps.title: Choose behaviours
steps.body: Choose the closest behaviour match for each selected element.
steps.meaning: Behaviours indicate likely points in a learning journey; they are not fixed levels.
steps.reassurance: If two behaviours seem close, pick the nearest fit to what the child can do independently.
steps.tip: Focus on demonstrated capability in this observation.

steps.title: Capture your notes
steps.body: Write a short, clear note about what the child did, said, or showed.
steps.meaning: This captures evidence of learning to support reflection and planning.
steps.reassurance: Simple is best; you do not need to analyse in this field.
steps.tip: Use observable facts and avoid interpretation.

steps.title: Review and finish
steps.body: Review your selections and notes, then save or export when ready.
steps.meaning: This step helps consolidate evidence and identify meaningful next teaching responses.
steps.reassurance: Your report is a living record and can be revisited.
steps.tip: Check that your evidence, behaviour choices, and next ideas align.
